'''''UFC 287: Pereira vs. Adesanya 2''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on April 8, 2023, at the [[Kaseya Center]] in [[Miami, Florida]], United States.<ref name="Miami">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/2/4/23585719/ufc-announces-miami-location-pereira-adesanya-masvidal-burns|title=UFC 287 headed to Miami for Pereira vs. Adesanya 2, Masvidal vs. Burns|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2023-02-04|access-date=2023-02-04}}</ref>

==Background==
The event marked the promotion's second visit to Miami and first since [[UFC 42]] in April 2003.<ref name="Miami" />

A [[UFC Middleweight Championship]] rematch between current champion [[Alex Pereira]] (also former [[Glory (kickboxing)#Glory Middleweight Championship|Glory Middleweight]] and [[Glory (kickboxing)#Glory Light Heavyweight Championship|Light Heavyweight Champion]]) and former champion [[Israel Adesanya]] headlined the event.<ref name="Fight">{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2023/01/27/ufc-287-gets-pereira-vs-adesanya-2-burns-vs-masvidal/|title=UFC 287 gets Pereira vs. Adesanya 2, Burns vs. Masvidal, Holland vs. Ponzinibbio|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Jay Anderson|date=2023-01-27|access-date=2023-01-27}}</ref> The pairing previously met at [[UFC 281]] in which Pereira captured the title by fifth-round knockout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cbssports.com/mma/news/ufc-281-results-takeaways-the-magic-of-madison-square-garden-delivers-again-in-an-all-time-event/amp/|title=UFC 281 results, takeaways: The magic of Madison Square Garden delivers again in an all-time event|publisher=cbssports.com|author=Brian Campbell|date=2022-11-13|access-date=2023-01-27}}</ref> The two also previously met twice in kickboxing matchups: the first in April 2016 at [[2016 in Glory of Heroes#Glory of Heroes 1|Glory of Heroes 1]], which Pereira won by unanimous decision, and their second at [[2017 in Glory of Heroes|Glory of Heroes 7]] in March 2017, which Pereira won by third-round knockout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmauk.net/2020/11/25/alex-pereira-and-israel-adesanya-an-undeniable-trilogy/|title=Alex Pereira and Israel Adesanya: an undeniable trilogy?|publisher=mmauk.net|author=Michael Owens|date=2020-11-25|access-date=2022-08-05}}</ref>

Nikolas Motta was expected to face [[Ignacio Bahamondes]] in a lightweight bout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/01/ignacio-bahamondes-vs-nikolas-motta-ufc-287-april-8-mma-fight-booking-matchup-announcement|title=Ignacio Bahamondes vs. Nikolas Motta on tap for UFC 287 in April|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2023-01-18|access-date=2023-03-26}}</ref> However, Motta pulled out in late March due to a cut on his head.<ref name="Baham">{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmanews.com/news/nikolas-motta-nasty-head-injury-ufc-287-withdrawal|title=Nikolas Motta withdraws from UFC 287 after brutal headbutt accident|publisher=mmanews.com|author=Curtis Calhoun|date=2023-03-24|access-date=2023-03-26}}</ref> He was replaced by Trey Ogden at a catchweight of 160 pounds.<ref name="Ogden">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/03/ufc-287-ignacio-bahamondes-vs-trey-ogden-mma-booking-matchup-nikolas-motta-out|title=With Nikolas Motta out, Ignacio Bahamondes now will face Trey Ogden at UFC 287|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun and Nolan King|date=2023-03-28|access-date=2023-03-28}}</ref>

A welterweight bout between [[The Ultimate Fighter: Live]] lightweight winner [[Michael Chiesa]] and [[Li Jingliang]] was expected to take place at the event.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.sherdog.com/news/news/Michael-ChiesaJingliang-Li-Added-to-UFC-287-Lineup-on-April-8-189089|title=Michael Chiesa-Jingliang Li added to UFC 287 lineup on April 8|publisher=sherdog.com|author=Jay Pretty|date=2023-02-04|access-date=2023-02-04}}</ref> However, Li withdrew from the bout due to a back injury and the bout was scrapped.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/03/ufc-287-news-li-jingliang-vs-michael-chiesa-injury|title=Li Jingliang out of UFC 287 fight vs. Michael Chiesa due to injury|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2023-03-27|access-date=2023-03-27}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2023/04/ufc-287-fight-card-news-michael-chiesa-out-li-jingliang|title=Michael Chiesa announces he's off UFC 287 after replacement options fizzle|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2023-04-03|access-date=2023-04-03}}</ref>

[[Chris Barnett]] was expected to face [[Chase Sherman]] in a heavyweight bout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2023/2/15/23600865/ufc-287-adds-chris-barnett-vs-chase-sherman-heavyweight-scrap|title=UFC 287 fight card: Chris Barnett vs. Chase Sherman heavyweight scrap headed to Miami|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Ryan Harkness|date=2023-02-15|access-date=2023-04-02}}</ref> However, Barnett pulled out due to undisclosed reasons in early April and was replaced by Karl Williams.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sportskeeda.com/mma/news-ufc-287-loses-heavyweight-matchup-new-opponent-s-name-reported|title=UFC 287 loses heavyweight matchup, new opponent's name reported|publisher=sportskeeda.com|author=Umar|date=2023-04-02|access-date=2023-04-02}}</ref> In turn, Sherman withdrew the day of the event due to a medical issue and the bout was scrapped.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/platform/amp/2023/4/8/23675723/karl-williams-vs-chase-sherman-pulled-from-ufc-287|title=Karl Williams vs. Chase Sherman pulled from UFC 287|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2023-04-08|access-date=2023-04-08}}</ref>

At the weigh-ins, Christian Rodriguez weighed in at 137 pounds, one pound over the bantamweight non-title fight limit. His bout proceeded at catchweight and he was fined 20% of his purse which went to his opponent [[Raul Rosas Jr.]]<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/platform/amp/2023/4/7/23674119/ufc-287-weigh-in-results-alex-pereira-israel-adesanya-main-event-grudge-match-fighter-misses-weight|title=UFC 287 weigh-in results: Alex Pereira, Israel Adesanya set for main event grudge match, one fighter misses weight|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2023-04-07|access-date=2023-04-07}}</ref>

During the event's broadcast, it was announced that a [[UFC Welterweight Championship]] bout that took place in July 2015 at [[UFC 189]] between then champion [[Robbie Lawler]] and challenger [[Rory MacDonald (fighter)|Rory MacDonald]] was the next "fight wing" [[UFC Hall of Fame]] inductee. Lawler defended the title via fifth-round TKO. It was also a rematch of a November 2013 fight in which Lawler also won (via split decision).<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2023/4/9/23675928/robbie-lawler-vs-rory-macdonald-clash-at-ufc-189-announced-for-ufc-hall-of-fame|title=Robbie Lawler vs. Rory MacDonald clash at UFC 189 announced for UFC Hall of Fame|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2023-04-09|access-date=2023-04-10}}</ref>

== Bonus awards ==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2023/4/9/23675588/ufc-287-official-bonus-winners-results-espn-mma-ppv-miami-adesanya-pereira-masvidal-font|title=UFC 287 bonuses: Rob Font leads $50K winners with stunning knockout|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Dan Hiergesell|date=2023-04-09|access-date=2023-04-09}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Kelvin Gastelum vs. Chris Curtis'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Israel Adesanya''' and '''Rob Font'''
